description: Routing discipline eval cases: should-trigger, should-not-trigger, and boundary cases for .NET skill loading

evals.prompt: I have a .NET 9 web API that's getting slow under load. The endpoint at /api/orders takes 3+ seconds when there are more than 1000 orders. Can you look at the EF Core queries and suggest optimizations?
evals.expected_output: Should invoke using-dotnet first, then dotnet-advisor, then route to dotnet-api (EF Core queries) with dotnet-csharp baseline loaded

evals.prompt: add a github actions workflow that builds and tests my csproj on every PR, with nuget caching
evals.expected_output: Should invoke using-dotnet first, then dotnet-advisor routing to dotnet-devops. The .csproj mention is a strong .NET signal even though the primary task is CI/CD

evals.prompt: Show me how to use records and pattern matching to model a payment state machine in C# with Approved, Declined, and Pending states
evals.expected_output: Should invoke using-dotnet then dotnet-advisor routing to dotnet-csharp. Pure language-level C# task

evals.prompt: help me set up a Blazor Server app with authentication using Microsoft Identity, I need login/logout and role-based access to an admin page
evals.expected_output: Should invoke using-dotnet then dotnet-advisor routing to dotnet-ui. Blazor + auth is clearly .NET UI territory

evals.prompt: I need to write integration tests for my ASP.NET minimal API using WebApplicationFactory. The API has JWT auth and I need to mock the token validation
evals.expected_output: Should invoke using-dotnet then dotnet-advisor routing to dotnet-testing. Testing with WebApplicationFactory is clearly .NET testing

evals.prompt: my app keeps crashing in production with an AccessViolationException. I have a dump file from procdump, how do I analyze it?
evals.expected_output: Should invoke using-dotnet then dotnet-advisor routing to dotnet-debugging. Crash dump analysis is .NET debugging territory

evals.prompt: I want to publish my console app as a single-file native AOT binary for linux-x64. What project settings do I need?
evals.expected_output: Should invoke using-dotnet then dotnet-advisor routing to dotnet-tooling. Native AOT publishing is build/tooling territory

evals.prompt: Build me a web app with .NET that processes orders and invoices
evals.expected_output: Should invoke using-dotnet then dotnet-advisor. Generic .NET web app request should route through advisor to determine the right domain skills

evals.prompt: I'm getting a NullReferenceException in my service layer. Here's the stack trace: at OrderService.ProcessAsync(Order order) in /src/Services/OrderService.cs:line 47
evals.expected_output: Should invoke using-dotnet. The .cs file path and .NET exception type are clear signals even though the user didn't say '.NET'

evals.prompt: can you look at my global.json and figure out why dotnet build is failing with SDK version mismatch
evals.expected_output: Should invoke using-dotnet. global.json and dotnet build are unambiguous .NET signals

evals.prompt: Write me a Python Flask API with SQLAlchemy that handles user registration and login with bcrypt password hashing
evals.expected_output: Should NOT invoke using-dotnet. This is explicitly Python/Flask, not .NET

evals.prompt: Help me configure my tsconfig.json and set up ESLint with Prettier for a React TypeScript project
evals.expected_output: Should NOT invoke using-dotnet. TypeScript/React frontend tooling, not .NET

evals.prompt: I need to write a Dockerfile for my Go microservice that does multi-stage builds and runs as non-root
evals.expected_output: Should NOT invoke using-dotnet. Go microservice, not .NET

evals.prompt: set up a postgres database with migrations using prisma and seed it with test data for my nextjs app
evals.expected_output: Should NOT invoke using-dotnet. Prisma/Next.js stack, not .NET even though database/migrations overlap conceptually

evals.prompt: What's the difference between async/await in JavaScript and C#? I'm coming from a Node.js background and trying to understand the .NET model
evals.expected_output: Should invoke using-dotnet. The user is asking about C#/.NET async specifically, even though JavaScript is mentioned for comparison
Should invoke using-dotnet. The code is clearly C# even though the user didn't mention .NET

evals.prompt: I need to set up dependency injection in my project. I'm using the built-in Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection container
evals.expected_output: Should invoke using-dotnet. Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection is explicitly .NET

evals.prompt: how do I read a JSON file in my application and deserialize it into an object?
evals.expected_output: Ambiguous - could be any language. Should NOT trigger .NET routing unless there are repo signals (.sln, .csproj files present). Without repo context, this is too generic

evals.prompt: help me optimize my database queries, they're really slow with large datasets and I think I need to add indexes
evals.expected_output: Ambiguous - database optimization is language-agnostic. Should NOT trigger .NET routing without repo signals

evals.prompt: I just created a new Uno Platform app and I need help setting up multi-targeting for iOS, Android, and WASM
evals.expected_output: Should invoke using-dotnet. Uno Platform is a .NET UI framework, should route to dotnet-ui
